title: bellevalia theraea
intro: Bellevalia theraea, also known as Thracian hyacinth, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. It is a perennial bulbous plant native to the Balkan Peninsula, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's roots and flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, offering health benefits like improved breathing and immune system support. Bellevalia theraea can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or extract, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
